Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 : Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" (or similar) to find the full NorthWind email history. Key discoveries: (a) Pam's original Denver relocation request detailing Raj Patel (server lab, hazmat, CISO chain-of-custody requirements from Victor Huang) and Emilia Cruz (Steinway Model B piano, 27-day lease overlap, April 18 hard deadline); (b) Victor Huang's security requirements email with five non-negotiable conditions (AES-256 verification, IT escort, direct transport, chain-of-custody log, GPS tracking); (c) Catalina's replies to Pam and Victor confirming coordination; (d) Catalina's internal email to David about Denver expansion risks; (e) Catalina's April 14 email to Pam (subject: "NorthWind service recovery plan by end of week") promising a comprehensive recovery plan covering the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breakdown and Emilia Cruz damage claim; (f) Pam's formal final-warning escalation (email_email_7168baed8438) sent to david.chen@moveops.com, CC victor.huang@northwindtech.com, stating NorthWind is evaluating whether MoveOps can continue and has begun conversations with PathFinder Logistics, requesting a retention proposal by EOD Friday; (g) David's internal "NorthWind retention package needed today" email requesting input from Chloe, Julian, Marcus, Alejandro, and Catalina. Also discover Q1 history: Pam's apartment quality complaints, her February escalation to David, David's $3,000 goodwill credit, and Pam's Q1 survey (5/10 overall, 4/10 communication, 5/10 service quality).
OE 2 : Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "Emilia Cruz" (or similar) to find internal discussions. Key discoveries: (a) Catalina's post in #operations (C006) detailing both Denver relocations and their complexity; (b) Chloe Vance's reply in #operations assigning Blessing Okafor and noting chain-of-custody is new for MoveOps; (c) Chloe's reconstructed ops timeline in #operations showing the chain-of-custody breach (crew arrived 7:00 AM, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, load proceeded without escort, equipment held overnight at SentinelChain warehouse); (d) Hana Kim's post in #finance (C005) about invoice INV-2026-0412 for $14,700; (e) Julian Brooks's note in #customer-support (C007) that Emilia Cruz called about the scratched Steinway wanting a callback on MoveOps vs KeyMove responsibility; (f) Blessing Okafor's field notes in #operations about the piano damage; (g) David's messages in #customer-engagement (C002) and #executive (C004) about NorthWind being the riskiest account, Catalina being overloaded, and PathFinder pitching in Denver; (h) Marcus Thorne's post in #finance about the $1,200 KeyMove insurance rider for the piano scratch.
OE 3 : Search for the NorthWind company record using crm_search_companies with name "NorthWind" (or similar) to find company_northwind. Key info: 150 employees, Manufacturing Tech, Chicago HQ, description noting quality/timeline issues and Catalina as AM. Then search deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "NorthWind" or company_id "company_northwind" to find: (a) deal_northwind (proposalMade, $60,000); (b) deal_northwind_won (closedWon, $60,000); (c) deal_northwind_denver_expansion (qualifiedToBuy, $14,700); (d) deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 (contractSent, $14,700). The agent should identify that the Denver expansion deal stage does not reflect reality: the contract is signed and work is in progress, yet the deal is still at qualifiedToBuy or contractSent.
OE 4 : Search CRM contacts using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Pam Kowalski" to find contact_pam_kowalski (email: pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, Director of HR Operations). Also search for Victor Huang, Raj Patel, and Emilia Cruz contacts. Then use crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_northwind" to find eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context, a comprehensive account recovery NOTE created 2026-04-01 covering the Denver expansion context, risk factors, and Q1 service history.
OE 5 : Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with query "NorthWind" or "retention" (or similar) to find: (a) linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 - "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" (priority 1, assigned to moveops_david_chen, team_operations, due 2026-04-24); (b) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind - "DOT Docs - Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS" (state_done, assigned to moveops_chloe_vance, project DOT compliance audit). Then read comments using linear_list_comments to find: (c) Chloe's comment (linear_comment_fc9fd7990fec) reconstructing the ops timeline (crew at 7 AM, escort for 9 AM, early load, SentinelChain overnight storage); (d) Chloe's comment (linear_comment_093d903c5cb9) flagging the DOT docs issue is marked Done but no signed custody log can be located, while the Airtable record still shows "In Progress"; (e) Alejandro's comment (linear_comment_b38c11307d3a) about a draft 12% Q3 retention discount model; (f) David's comment (linear_comment_dd1f4bceee90) framing NorthWind as a combined control failure (Raj) and care failure (Emilia) amplified by Catalina's missed April 14 promise and stale CRM records.
OE 6 : Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (field_name "Company", value "NorthWind Technologies") or airtable_list_records (table_name "Relocations", base_id "appMoveOpsOps001") to find: (a) recRajPatelChicagoDenver - Raj Patel, Chicago to Denver, Status "In Progress", Move Start 2026-04-21, Move End 2026-04-23, Coordinator Blessing Okafor, special requirements for server lab, hazmat, chain-of-custody, IT escort; (b) recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver - Emilia Cruz, Chicago to Denver, Status "In Progress", Move Start 2026-04-14, Move End 2026-04-18, Coordinator Blessing Okafor, special requirements for Steinway piano, multi-vendor coordination, lease overlap; (c) recRajPatelAuditNote0411 - audit trail noting escort confirmed for 9:00 AM but movers arrived at 7:00 AM and loaded without waiting. The agent should identify both Denver records still show "In Progress" despite the physical moves being executed.
OE 7 : Search invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "NorthWind" (or similar) to find: (a) inv_northwind_01 (INV-2026-003) - $60,000 Q1 invoice for 5 enterprise relocations billed to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, Balance $60,000; (b) inv_northwind_denver_02 (INV-2026-0412) - $14,700 Denver expansion invoice with line items: Raj Patel standard relocation $4,500, hazmat surcharge $1,800, chain-of-custody protocol $1,200 (PROVISIONAL, pending Swift confirmation), Emilia Cruz standard relocation $4,500, specialty piano moving $2,200 (PROVISIONAL, pending subcontractor agreement), early lease termination negotiation $500. Total $14,700, due 2026-05-01. The agent should flag the provisional items as potential billing surprises for Pam.
OE 8 : Search bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "KeyMove" or "piano" (or similar) to find BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 - a $1,200 insurance claim rider from KeyMove Specialty Transport for the Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction, due 2026-04-24. This is additional cost exposure tied to the piano damage.
OE 9 : Check calendar availability for early this week using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to or calendar_read_today_calendar_events to find a suitable slot for a three-way call between David, Catalina, and Pam.
OE 10 : Update both Denver relocation records using airtable_update_records (base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id "tblRelocations01") to reflect reality: (a) update recRajPatelChicagoDenver Status from "In Progress" to "Completed" (physical move executed despite chain-of-custody breach); (b) update recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver Status from "In Progress" to "Completed" (move completed despite piano damage). Notes about incidents should be added so Pam cannot reference stale records against the team.
OE 11 : Update the NorthWind Denver expansion deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage. The deal (deal_northwind_denver_expansion or deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2) is at qualifiedToBuy or contractSent, but the contract is signed and work has been executed. The agent should move the deal to reflect reality (e.g., closedWon or similar).
OE 12 : Log a comprehensive account summary on David's tracking ticket using linear_create_comment with issueId "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1". The comment should consolidate: Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach details, Emilia Cruz piano damage and missed callback, Catalina's undelivered April 14 recovery plan, billing status (provisional items, KeyMove claim), Airtable and CRM corrections made, PathFinder threat, and upcoming Pam call.
OE 13 : Schedule a call with Pam early this week using calendar_add_calendar_event with title related to "NorthWind account review" (or similar), attendees david.chen@moveops.com, catalina.dubois@moveops.com, and pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, a start_datetime early in the week, and a description covering the call purpose.
OE 14 : Send a heads-up email to Catalina using send_email (sender david.chen@moveops.com, recipient catalina.dubois@moveops.com) briefing her on the NorthWind situation before the Pam call: the Raj Patel breach, Emilia Cruz piano damage and missed follow-up, Pam's formal escalation mentioning PathFinder, key issues Pam will raise (Catalina's undelivered April 14 recovery plan), and the scheduled call details.
OE 15 : Send an email to Pam using send_email (sender david.chen@moveops.com, recipient pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com). The email should own what went wrong with the Denver moves (be direct about the Raj Patel handling failure and Emilia Cruz piano damage and communication gap), not rehash Q1 unless Pam raised it (her escalation focuses on Denver incidents), propose concrete next steps including the scheduled call, and convey personal accountability.
OE 16 : Post a heads-up in #customer-engagement (channel C002) using conversations_add_message so Mina Hashimoto and Emeka Diallo are not caught off guard. The message should summarize: Pam's formal escalation, Denver move failures (Raj Patel breach, Emilia Cruz piano damage), PathFinder competitive threat, and the upcoming call with Pam.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1 : Search emails using search_emails with a query related to "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" (or similar) to find the full NorthWind email history. Key discoveries: (a) Pam's original Denver relocation request detailing Raj Patel (server lab, hazmat, CISO chain-of-custody requirements from Victor Huang) and Emilia Cruz (Steinway Model B piano, 27-day lease overlap, April 18 hard deadline); (b) Victor Huang's security requirements email with five non-negotiable conditions (AES-256 verification, IT escort, direct transport, chain-of-custody log, GPS tracking); (c) Catalina's replies to Pam and Victor confirming coordination; (d) Catalina's internal email to David about Denver expansion risks; (e) Catalina's April 14 email to Pam (subject: "NorthWind service recovery plan by end of week") promising a comprehensive recovery plan covering the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breakdown and Emilia Cruz damage claim; (f) Pam's formal final-warning escalation (email_email_7168baed8438) sent to david.chen@moveops.com, CC victor.huang@northwindtech.com, stating NorthWind is evaluating whether MoveOps can continue and has begun conversations with PathFinder Logistics, requesting a retention proposal by EOD Friday; (g) David's internal "NorthWind retention package needed today" email requesting input from Chloe, Julian, Marcus, Alejandro, and Catalina. Also discover Q1 history: Pam's apartment quality complaints, her February escalation to David, David's $3,000 goodwill credit, and Pam's Q1 survey (5/10 overall, 4/10 communication, 5/10 service quality).
OE 2 : Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "Emilia Cruz" (or similar) to find internal discussions. Key discoveries: (a) Catalina's post in #operations (C006) detailing both Denver relocations and their complexity; (b) Chloe Vance's reply in #operations assigning Blessing Okafor and noting chain-of-custody is new for MoveOps; (c) Chloe's reconstructed ops timeline in #operations showing the chain-of-custody breach (crew arrived 7:00 AM, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, load proceeded without escort, equipment held overnight at SentinelChain warehouse); (d) Hana Kim's post in #finance (C005) about invoice INV-2026-0412 for $14,700; (e) Julian Brooks's note in #customer-support (C007) that Emilia Cruz called about the scratched Steinway wanting a callback on MoveOps vs KeyMove responsibility; (f) Blessing Okafor's field notes in #operations about the piano damage; (g) David's messages in #customer-engagement (C002) and #executive (C004) about NorthWind being the riskiest account, Catalina being overloaded, and PathFinder pitching in Denver; (h) Marcus Thorne's post in #finance about the $1,200 KeyMove insurance rider for the piano scratch.
OE 3 : Search for the NorthWind company record using crm_search_companies with name "NorthWind" (or similar) to find company_northwind. Key info: 150 employees, Manufacturing Tech, Chicago HQ, description noting quality/timeline issues and Catalina as AM. Then search deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "NorthWind" or company_id "company_northwind" to find: (a) deal_northwind (proposalMade, $60,000); (b) deal_northwind_won (closedWon, $60,000); (c) deal_northwind_denver_expansion (qualifiedToBuy, $14,700); (d) deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 (contractSent, $14,700). OE 4 : Search CRM contacts using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Pam Kowalski" to find contact_pam_kowalski (email: pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, Director of HR Operations). Also search for Victor Huang, Raj Patel, and Emilia Cruz contacts. Then use crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_northwind" to find eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context, a comprehensive account recovery NOTE created 2026-04-01 covering the Denver expansion context, risk factors, and Q1 service history.
OE 5 : Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with query "NorthWind" or "retention" (or similar) to find: (a) linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 - "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" (priority 1, assigned to moveops_david_chen, team_operations, due 2026-04-24); (b) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind - "DOT Docs - Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS" (state_done, assigned to moveops_chloe_vance, project DOT compliance audit). OE 10 : Update both Denver relocation records using airtable_update_records (base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id "tblRelocations01") to reflect reality: (a) update recRajPatelChicagoDenver Status from "In Progress" to "Completed" (physical move executed despite chain-of-custody breach); (b) update recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver Status from "In Progress" to "Completed" (move completed despite piano damage). 📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The Raj Patel Denver relocation record (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) was updated (via airtable_update_records) with Status changed to \\"Completed\\" (or equivalent reflecting the move was physically executed).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows airtable_update_records was called with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001', table_id 'tblRelocations01', and a record entry for 'recRajPatelChicagoDenver' with Status set to 'Completed -- Service Failure'.
✓Run #2The airtable_update_records call explicitly includes recRajPatelChicagoDenver with Status set to 'Completed' in the records array, using base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_id 'tblRelocations01'.
✓Run #3airtable_update_records was called with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001', table_id 'tblRelocations01', and included record recRajPatelChicagoDenver with 'Status': 'Completed'.
✓Run #4The airtable_update_records call includes recRajPatelChicagoDenver with Status set to 'Completed - Service Failure', confirmed by the tool result in tool_call_result_18.json.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email content to catalina.dubois@moveops.com explicitly states: 'Raj Patel: SentinelChain's crew arrived at 7 AM on April 10 and loaded the servers before the 9 AM IT escort showed up. The shipment was then placed in overnight warehouse storage — both of Victor's non-negotiable requirements were violated. No signed chain-of-custody log was ever collected.'
✓Run #2The email to catalina.dubois@moveops.com content explicitly references the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach: 'the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and the Emilia Cruz piano damage with the missed callback'.
✓Run #3The email to Catalina explicitly references 'Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach: SentinelChain loaded the servers two hours before Victor's IT escort arrived' and describes the security protocol failure in detail.
✓Run #4The email to Catalina explicitly mentions 'SentinelChain's crew started loading two hours before the IT escort arrived' and the overnight warehouse storage violation — the core elements of the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach.
✓Run #5The email to catalina.dubois@moveops.com includes a dedicated section 'RAJ PATEL — What happened with chain of custody' describing the crew arriving early, loading before escort, and overnight storage.
✓Run #6The email to catalina.dubois@moveops.com explicitly states: 'SentinelChain loaded before the escort arrived (7 AM vs. 9 AM confirmed arrival), then routed the shipment to overnight warehouse storage — both explicitly prohibited.' The escort mismatch and chain-of-custody breach are clearly described.
12 The email to Pam (visible in send_email content parameter) acknowledges the communication gap on the Emilia Cruz case (e.g., missed callback or delayed follow-up after the piano damage was reported).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The email to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com explicitly acknowledges the missed callback: 'Emilia had to chase us for a response. She asked for a same-day callback and didn't get one.'
✓Run #3The email to Pam states 'Emilia should not have had to chase us for accountability' and 'I am putting together a clear resolution path', directly acknowledging the communication failure and missed callback.
✓Run #4The email to Pam states 'Emilia asked for a same-day callback and we didn't get back to her. She should never have had to chase us for accountability on something we caused. That communication failure is on us.' — directly acknowledging the missed callback.
✓Run #5The email to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com explicitly states: 'Emilia Cruz should not have had to chase us after her piano was damaged. She asked for a same-day callback and did not receive one. That is a basic accountability failure on our part.'
✓Run #6The email to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com references 'the damage to Emilia's piano and the communication gap that followed', directly acknowledging the missed callback/communication failure regarding the Emilia Cruz case.
